ShopItems.Description: Fast travel to any map coordinate.

RoomEncounters.Description: "You fight the silly little man, You have legit no reason to, you are just kind of a terrible person all around, but at least he won't bother you anymore."
"You have encountered a skeleton! He is a silly bag of bones, I think he's even falling apart. There isn't much he can do against a good old cardiovascular system"
"You have encountered a rat! He look's kind of small though, I'm sure you can take him, realistically if you die here, you were never meant to play this game"

" This spider is actually terrifying, but he's still just a spider right? RIGHT? There's no way you'll lose to a spider? You're meant to be the savior of Dawnbarrow not the sissy who ran from danger."
I hope it's clear now that you should only be using this part of the game as an exploration tool, and that by NO means should you search on the ground after defeating this zombie
There is a ghoul here who has 4 peg legs for appendages, he doesn't seem threatening in the slightest but where did these pegs come from

This snake is no ordinary snake, he seems slimier than usual and has extra rows of fangs? I'm not sure the species, but I can tell you one thing, this creature looks like the love child of a snake and a florida man!
An evil river monster appears, and he is not up for small chat
Spooky Scary Skeletons send shivers down my spine
This ghoul looks hungry for cats, you can hear him purring as cats do, I think there is only ONE thing to do here, put this sick monster out of it's misery.

An amalgamation of different creatures you're sure you've seen before are melded into one super creature. Staring in its many faces crying in agony, you notice that this creature has as many legs, tails, and arms as it does eyes.

*This creature is a hulking mass of 3 heads, it's clear that he doesn't like you, and that each head functions independently of each other, almost as if they were 3 mean dogs that were forced to be together for all of eternity.

The menacing Prarie Dog has an irrational anger towards you for laughing at his brethren. There's something caught around his neck! His razor sharp fangs look deadly and are best dealt with using a blade of your own!
We're getting really creative with these monster names are we not?
Wow, how original, a sea creature, but it's BIGGER
This creature COULD have been a Rock Lobster, but it seems like he is fixated on NOT being a rock lobster
Anyone can be a Rock Lobster if they set their mind to it. Be a little careful here
You really want that pickaxe, and the only thing standing between you and that pickaxe is this goblin!
*The hydra is unafraid of you, and even though he is stuck in the ocean, his reach feels MUCH longer, as if he could pick up the entire beach and throw it at you.
Nice try trying to fight here you COWARD, you must feel really proud of yourself, I bet you can beat this other COWARD
***The signpost @ the fork in the road reads "Only cowards take the southern path"

*THIS IS IT, THE FINAL FIGHT, WELCOME TO THE THUNDERDOME, THIS GIGANTIC DRAGON IS THE HECKIN SCARIEST THING KNOWN TO MAN, BE CAREFUL BRAVE ADVENTURER. IN THE CLEAREST SPOT OF THIS ROOM IS AN ANTI-CAT SIGN

*This humongous creature could not look any more sad. He seems to be guarding a piece of the saviors armor, you're more than welcome to try and fight him, but people this sad need friends, and even this monster doesn't deserve this fate

ExploreEncounterProfiles.Description: Something small but mean rustles in the brush and lunges at you.
The thick jungle stirs and something ugly comes crawling out.
The water breaks and a wandering river creature takes interest in you.
Something skulking through the tall grass decides you look worth bothering.
Your exploring wakes up something that should have stayed hidden.

EnemySkills.MessageTemplate: {enemy} exhales {skill} for {damage} damage and sets you ablaze!
{enemy} uses {skill} for {damage} damage and inflicts bleed!
{enemy} spits venom for {damage} damage and worsening bleed!

CommandUsage.Usage:
Usage: north | n
When: Move one tile north when not in combat.

Usage: south | s
When: Move one tile south when not in combat.

Usage: east | e
When: Move one tile east when not in combat.

Usage: west | w
When: Move one tile west when not in combat.

Usage: fight
When: Start combat with the current room enemy.

Usage: hit
When: Basic attack after combat has started.

Usage: buy <item>
Examples: buy fire bomb, buy scroll of freezing, buy mana.

Usage: use <item>
Examples: use fire bomb, use scroll of freezing, use scroll of fire.

Usage: use scroll of flight x y
Example: use scroll of flight 5 2
When: Fast travel while out of combat.

Usage: save OR save <filename>
Examples: save, save run42

Usage: load OR load <filename>
Examples: load, load run42

Usage: cheat
When: Testing only. Grants an extreme overpowered loadout.
